intro:
There is no information found on "Santia × littoralis". However, I found information on Santalum spicatum and Santalum album which both are known by the name Sandalwood or Santal.

Here is a paragraph about Santalum spicatum, also known as Western Australian Sandalwood or Santal:

Santalum spicatum, known as Western Australian Sandalwood or Santal, is a species of sandalwood tree native to Western Australia. The essential oil extracted from its heartwood is rich in bioactive constituents such as santalol, santal, and other terpenoids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Santalum spicatum include its ability to soothe skin irritations, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote a good night's sleep. The most common herbal preparations made with Santalum spicatum include essential oils, extracts, and tinctures, which are used in aromatherapy, skincare products, and traditional medicine.
